title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5 Transaction Entries
text: 1. This chapter deals with the transaction entries that are to be passed under Double Entry Accrual accounting system. These are representative entries and needs to be modified on a basis of actual transaction during the year. 2. The transaction entries covers the following areas in the Manual: (i) Identification of the appropriate ledger account and weather it has to be debited or credited (ii) Identification of the code from the Chart of Accounts (iii) Explanation to the entry (iv) Identification of the transaction or other recognisable event i.e. trigger point. (v) Identification of source document for the transaction (vi) Records to be updated 3 In this section, the representative accounting entries are shown as illustrations which can act as a guide to the user of this Manual.

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.1 Property Tax
text: 1. INTRODUCTION 1.1 Property Tax (including Surcharge) is the main source of Tax revenue for ULB's. Property Tax is assessed by the ULB's either on the basis of valuations received from West Bengal Valuation Board (WBVB) or internal valuations. 1.2 In the case of accounting for Property Tax, while the Account books are updated, the customer Accounts are also updated by a parallel operation. For e.g. when a Bill is paid by the assessee, the same is first entered in the Cash Book, which will be reflected in the General Ledger. Simultaneously based on the challan, the customer Account is also updated in the Demand and Collection Register. Thus, for the same receipt, entry is made in two sets of books, the Account book (acting as Control Account in the form of Ledger) and the Customer A/c (acting as Subsidiary Ledger in the form of Demand and Collection Register). 1.3 In ULBs, the accounting Ledger (Control Account/ Primary Accounting Code) is maintained by the Accounts Department, while the customer Ledger (subsidiary Account/ Secondary Accounting Code) is maintained by the Tax Department. The Financial Statements are to be prepared, based on the Control Account maintained by the Accounts Department. 1.4 This section deals with accounting entries for the Control Account, i.e. the entries given below are on consolidated basis. Frequent reconciliation of the Control Account with the subsidiary Accounts maintained by the Tax Department should be carried out to reconcile the balances. In case there are differences in balances, rectification of either book should be carried out to reconcile the balance. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES Recording of Demand raised in case of new assessments Debit 431-10-01 Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings (Current Year) Debit 431-10-03 Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings (Current Year) Debit 431-10-13 Surcharge receivable on Property Tax (Current year) Credit 110-01-01 To Property Tax from Residential buildings Credit 110-01-02 To Property Tax from Commercial buildings Credit 110-01-07 To Surcharge on Property Tax Explanation: The entry shall be passed quarterly, on the basis of the quarterly demand and is to be passed only for the current demand, i.e. excluding Arrears. The above entry shows recording of demand for residential and commercial buildings, and surcharge on the same. Trigger Point: On a quarterly basis either on an entry in the Assessment Register (Form 5) or raising of the Bill, whichever is earlier. Source Document: Form - 5 Assessment Register, Form N-109 Demand Register , Summary Statement of Bills raised Form N-128 Records Updated: Journal book, Ledger, Control Account for each of the source of Property Tax 2 Recording of Demand raised in case of new assessments Debit 431-10-01 Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings (Current Year) Debit 431-10-03 Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings (Current Year) Debit 431-10-13 Surcharge receivable on Property Tax (Current year) Credit 110-01-01 To Property Tax from Residential buildings Credit 110-01-02 To Property Tax from Commercial buildings Credit 110-01-07 To Surcharge on Property Tax Credit 280-10-01 To Prior Period Income- Property Tax Explanation: In case of new assessments, demand may be raised for a period earlier to the financial year in which the assessment is made. In such case the demand should be split according to the period to which it relates and income related to earlier period will be accounted as Prior Period Income. The above entry shows recording of demand for new assessments in case of residential and commercial buildings, and surcharge on the same. Trigger Point: As and when new assessments are done and recorded in the Assessment Register in Form no-5 or Bill is raised, whichever is earlier Source Document: Form no-5 Assessment Register, Form N-109 Demand Register, Summary statement of Bills raised Form N-128 Records Updated: Journal book, Ledger, Control Account for each of the source Recording of Change in Assessments (i) Revised demand being more than the earlier demand (cid:183) In case the revised demand is more than what was recorded earlier, then, the entry to be passed is same as accounting of existing demand, for the difference amount i.e. revised demand less earlier demand, as described in entry number 1 above. (ii) Revised demand being less than the earlier demand Debit 110-90-01 Tax remissions and refunds-Property Tax Debit 280-50-01 Prior period expenses- Refund of Property Tax Credit 431-10-01 To Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings (CurrentYear) Credit 431-10-03 To Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings (Current Year) Credit 431-10-13 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax (Current Year) Explanation: If the revision of demand relates to the current year, then the accounting code, 110-90-01 will be debited; if the revision of demand relates to the previous years, then the accounting code 280-50-01 will be debited. If the demand relates to current year as well as previous year, then the amount will be debited as per actual figures, to both the Accounts. In case the revision is of earlier years as well, the Arrears Receivable Account will be credited. The remission/ refund amount shall be adjusted against Receivables only if the demand is outstanding. The above entry shows recording of change in assessments for residential and commercial buildings, and surcharge on the same. Trigger Point: As and when revised assessments are done and recorded in the Assessment Register in Form -5 Source Document: Form -5 Assessment Register, Form N 109 Demand Register, Summary statement of Bills raised Form N 128 Records Updated: Journal book, Control Account for each of the source of the Property Tax and Ledger Account (iii) Revised demand being less than the earlier demand, and the demand has already being paid. Debit 110-90-01 Tax remissions and refunds-Property Tax Debit 280-50-01 Prior period expenses- Refund of Property Tax Credit 350-41-01 To Advance Collection of Property Tax Explanation: If the revision of demand relates to the current year, then the accounting code, 110-90-01 will be debited; if the revision of demand relates to the previous years then the accounting code 280-50-01 will be debited. If the demand relates to current year as well as previous year, then the amount to be debited will be the actual amount. If the demand has already being paid, then the same will be taken as Credit to Advance collection of Property Tax and adjusted against future receivables. Trigger Point: As and when new assessments are done and recorded in the Assessment Register in Form -5 Source Document: Form -5 Assessment Register, Form N 109 Demand Register, Summary statement of Bills raised Form N 128. Records Updated: Journal book, Control Account for each of the source of the Property Tax and Ledger Account (iv) Adjustment of Advance collection of Property Tax Debit 350-41-01 Advance Collection of Property Tax Credit 431-10-01 To Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings (Current Year) Credit 431-10-03 To Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings (Current Year) Credit 431-10-13 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax (Current Year) Explanation: The Advance collection of Property Tax will be transferred to the receivable Account of the Property Tax, when Tax for the current year falls due. The above entry shows adjustment of Advance collection of Property Tax against receivables for residential and commercial buildings and Surcharge on the same for current year. Trigger Point: After passing the entry for Accrual of income every quarter, the Advance shall be adjusted against the demand Source Document: Form -5 Assessment Register, Form N 109 Demand Register, Summary statement of Bills raised Form N 128. Records Updated: Journal book, Control Account for each of the source of the receivables for the Property Tax and Ledger Account 5 Collection of Property Tax Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 431-80-01 To Receivable Control A/c - Property Tax Explanation: The Summary of Daily Collection (N 105) does not provide the details in respect of the year-wise head-wise collections made in respect of Property and Other Taxes. Hence, the total amount collected should be credited to "Receivables Control Accounts - Property Taxes" Account. The collection made shall be segregated into year wise, head wise collection on a monthly basis. Trigger Point: Receipt of cash/cheques from the individual assessee/ Collecting Sarkar. Source Document: Form N 109 Demand Register, Daily Collection Challan as per Form 16, Cash Receipts, Tax Register/ Receipt Book of the Collecting Sarkar. Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger 6 Recording of break-up of collections Debit 431-80-01 Receivable Control A/c -Property Tax Credit 431-10-01 To Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings- Current year Credit 431-10-02 To Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings- Arrears Credit 431-10-03 To Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings-Current year Credit 431-10-04 To Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings-Arrears Credit 431-10-13 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax-Current Credit 431-10-14 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax-Arrear Credit 350-41-01 To Advance Collection of revenues-Property Tax Credit 171-80-02 To Other interest-Interest on Property Tax receivable Credit 140-20-03 To Penalties and Fines under West Bengal Municipal Act, 1993 Explanation: The Receivable Control A/c will be transferred to the different sources for the Property Tax (Illustration showing for the residential and commercial buildings) along with Interest, Fines and Penalties collected along with Property Tax Trigger Point: At the end of the month for all the collections made during the month Source Document: Demand Register as per Form 109 , Daily Collection Challan as per Form-16, Summary Statement of Year-wise, Head-wise collection of Property & Other Taxes as per Form N-130 Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger 7 When cheque banked by the cashier is dishonoured Debit 431-80-01 Receivable Control A/c -Property Tax Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: When notice of the cheques dishonoured is received by the cashier and entry for "Recording of break-up of collections" as above has not been passed, the above entry will be passed. However in case the "Recording of break- up of collections" entry has been passed, the respective Receivable Account will be debited. Trigger Point: As soon as intimation is received from the Bank Source Document: Intimation letter from Bank, Bank reconciliation statement, Dishonoured cheque Register Records Updated: Bank Book, Ledger, Register of Dishonoured cheques and Drafts (Form N-103) Discount and Remissions Debit 110-90-01 Tax remissions and refund Property Tax Debit 240-80-01 Other Finance Expenses- Discounts on early/ prompt payments Credit 431-10-01 To Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings-Current year Credit 431-10-02 To Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings-Arrears Credit 431-10-03 To Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings-Current year Credit 431-10-04 To Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings-Arrears Credit 431-10-13 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax-Current Credit 431-10-14 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax-Arrear Explanation: The entry will be passed when the Remission/Rebate has been approved by the Mayor/ Chairman or Assessment Review Committee. When remission is granted only that portion of the remission will be accounted by the above entry. The above entry shows adjustment of Discount and Remission against receivables for residential and commercial buildings and Surcharge on the same. Trigger Point: When the revised Bill is raised. Source Document: Letter of the Mayor/ Chairman/ Review Committee approving remission or raising of the revised Bill, Register of Remission orders( Form -21) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger 9 Recording of Notice Fees and Warrant Fees Debit 431-10-01 Property Tax receivable on Residential Buildings - Current Year Debit 431-10-03 Property Tax receivable on Commercial Buildings - Current Year Credit 140-40-08 To Other Fees-Notice Fees Credit 140-40-09 To Other Fees-Warrant Fees Explanation: The Notice fees and Warrant fees to be received shall be debited to the respective source of Property Tax (Illustration showing Notice and Warrant Fees receivable for residential and commercial buildings) and a consolidated entry shall be passed Trigger Point: At the end of the month for all Notices and Warrants sent during the Month Source Document: Form N-113 showing summary Statement of Notice Fee, Warrant Fee and Other Fees Records Updated: Journal book, Ledger and Receivable Account of the Sources of the Property Tax, Notice Fees and Warrant Fees 10 Remissions and Refunds (for tax already collected) Debit 110-90-01 Tax remissions and refunds-Property Tax Debit 280-50-01 Prior Period Expenses-Refund of Taxes-Property Taxes Credit 350-41-01 To Advance Collection of Revenue-Property Tax Explanation: In case demand is outstanding for Property Tax receivables, the accounting entry for remissions and refunds that should be passed shall be as detailed out in Sl. no 8. Refunds/ Remissions pertaining to prior period shall be identified separately from current periods and debited as Prior Period Expenses. The balance that has to be refunded shall be credited to Advance collection of Taxes Account. Trigger Point: At the end of the month for all remissions and refunds during that month Source Document: Summary statement of remissions and refunds in Form N- 115, Form 21- Register of remission orders Records Updated: Journal book, Ledger 11 Provision for doubtful recovery of Property Tax Debit 270-10-01 Provisions for Doubtful Receivables-Property Taxes Credit 432-10-04 To Provision for outstanding Property Tax-Sick and Closed Industries Explanation: Provision for Property Tax, in case of Sick and Closed Industries shall be made for 100% of the outstanding Demand Trigger Point: At the time of making the provision, when the industry is declared sick or closed and information of the same is received by the ULB Source Document: Letter of intimation, Letter/ Minutes of meeting of Board of Councillors approving provision, Computation sheet for the amount of provisioning Records Updated: Journal book, Ledger Account 12 Recording of write- offs Debit 432-10-04 Provision for outstanding Property Tax-Sick and Closed Industries Credit 431-10-09 To Property Tax receivable on Sick and Closed Industries-(Current Year) Credit 431-10-10 To Property Tax receivable on Sick and Closed Industries-(Arrears) Credit 431-10-13 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax (Current year) Credit 431-10-14 To Surcharge receivable on Property Tax (Arrears) Explanation: In case the receivable from Property Tax is to be written off and has been decided by the ULB and approval of the Government is received, which was earlier treated as an income and provided for, the write off shall be adjusted against the provision made and the above entry shall be passed. Trigger Point: At the time of writing off the income Source Document: Letter of intimation to write-off/ Minutes of meeting of Board of Councillors, Approval letter from the Government, Property tax- Summary Statement of write offs(N-132) Records Updated: Journal book, Ledger 13 Recording of subsequent collection/ recovery of income written off Debit 431-80-01 Receivable Control Account Property Taxes Credit 280-30-01 To Recovery of Revenues written off Tax Revenue Explanation: In case of a 'Receivable of Property & Other Taxes' written off already is recovered/ collected during the year; the above entry shall be passed by the Accounts Department as an adjustment entry. Trigger Point: At the time of recovery of revenue earlier written off Source Document: Form N 109 Demand Register, Daily Collection Challan as per Form 16 Records Updated: Journal book, Control Account of the Property Tax receivable, Ledger Account RECOVERY OF PROPERTY AND OTHER TAXES THROUGH LEGAL PROCEEDINGS Recording the cost of recovery Debit 220-51-02 Legal Expenses-Cost of recoveries of Tax revenue Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: Expenditure incurred in respect of attachment and sale of properties of the defaulter, shall be recorded as cost incurred for recovery. Trigger Point: At the time of Issuing Cheque Source Document: Bill of the Professionals, Other Bills Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account Recording of collections on sale of Attached Property Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 350-90-04 To Sale Proceeds Attached Properties Explanation: On sale of the attached Property, the total amount shall be credited to the Sale Proceeds Account. Trigger Point: At the time of receipt of the sale proceeds Source Document: Summary of Daily Collection (Form No. N105) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 16 Recording of break up of sale proceeds Debit 350-90-04 Sale Proceeds-Attached Properties Credit 431-80-01 To Receivable Control Accounts-Property Taxes Credit 350-30-03 To Government Dues Payables-Court Attachments Credit 350-40-04 To Refunds Payable-Attached Properties Explanation: The Tax Department shall send the details of the adjustment of the amount collected to the Accounts Department. Based on the intimation received from the Tax Department, to record the adjustment of the amount collected on sale of attached Property, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Expenses incurred to recover the Property shall be credited to Receivable Control Account-Property Tax A/c. Trigger Point: Receipt of adjustment of sale proceeds from the Accounts Department. Ledger Account of the sale proceeds should not have balance at the year end. Source Document: Intimation from the Tax Department with regard to the Tax adjustments, Demand Register (Form N 109) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 17 Payment of the Dues Debit 350-30-03 Government Dues Payables-Court Attachments Debit 350-40-04 Refunds Payable-Attached Properties Credit 450-21-01 To Bank Account Explanation: The Tax Department shall prepare the Payment Orders (Form N 106) in respect of the amounts payable subsequent to the sale of the attached Property and send it to the Accounts Department for payment. Trigger Point: Receipt of Payment order from the Tax Department and drawing of cheque. Source Document: Intimation from the Tax Department with regard to the Payment Order Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 17 Recording of break-up of the receivable Control Account The entry for recording of break up of the receivable Control Account shall be same as detailed above in Entry number 6. 19 Recording for receipt of cost of recovery Debit 431-80-01 Receivable Control accounts-Property Taxes Credit 220-51-02 To Legal Expenses-Cost of recoveries of Tax revenue Explanation: The legal Expenses incurred earlier and booked as expenditure, shall be transferred from the Receivable Control Account and reduced from expenditure. Trigger Point: After the payment has been made for the legal expenses Source Document: Professional's Bill, Other Bills Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.2 Advertisement Tax
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section deals with the accounting transactions, related to collection of Advertisement Tax by ULBs. 1.2 Advertisement Tax is generally collected in Advance by the ULBs and license is issued only thereafter. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Recording of approval of license form for advertisements Debit 431-10-01 Advertisement Tax receivable on Land Hoarding (Current Year) Debit 431-10-03 Advertisement Tax receivable on Bus Shelter (Current Year) Credit 110-11-01 To Advertisement Tax Land hoarding Credit 110-11-02 To Advertisement Tax Bus Shelter Explanation: The entry shall be passed as and when licenses are approved for advertisements or contract is signed. (Illustration shown for Advertisement Tax on Land hoardings and Bus Shelter) Trigger Point: As per the Agreement when demand is accrued Source Document: Form - 28 Register of Advertisement Tax Records Updated: Journal, Ledger 2 The other entries for collection of Advertisement Tax, provisioning, discount and remission, notice and warrant fees, recovery through legal way, etc. shall be similar to as described in the section on Property Tax.

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.3 Water Supply Revenue
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section deals with the accounting transactions, related to water supply functions of the ULBs. 1.2 The following are the revenue sources in relation to water supply i. Water supply charges ii. Sewerage charges iii. Meter rent for water supply iv. Connection charges for water supply v. Water tanker charges, and vi. Road damage recovery charges. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Raising of demand Debit 431-30-03 Receivables for Fees & User Charge-Water Supply (Current Year) Credit 140-50-12 To User Charges-Water Supply Credit 140-50-08 To User Charges-Sewerage Clearance Charges Credit 140-50-16 To User Charges-Meter rent for water Supply Explanation: On the basis of Summary Statement of Bills Raised, which is prepared ward-wise and income - head-wise in Form N 124 on a monthly basis within 7 days from the end of the previous month. Trigger Point: At the start of each month on the basis of monthly demand Source Document: Demand Register (Form N 109), Water Supply-Summary Statement of demand raised on assessment ( Form N 133) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 2 Collection of water supply income Debit 450-21-01 Bank Account Credit 431-80-07 To Receivable Control Accoun -Water Supply Explanation: Based on the Summary of Daily Collection (N 105) received from various Collecting Offices/Centers, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Trigger Point: End of the day on Receipt of Daily Collection Statement Source Document: Demand and Collection Register, Daily Collection statement Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 3 Break up of collection of water supply income Debit 431-80-07 Receivable Control Account-Water Supply Credit 431-30-03 To Receivables for Fees & User Charges-Water Supply(Current year) Credit 431-30-04 To Receivables for Fees & User Charges-Water Supply (Arrears) Credit 350-41-06 To Advance Collection of Revenues-Water Supply Explanation: The Summary of Daily collection (Form N 105) does not provide the details in respect of the year-wise head-wise collections made in respect of Water Supply. The collection made shall be segregated into year wise, head wise collection on a monthly basis and maintained as 'Water Supply-Summary statement of year-wise head-wise collection of Water Taxes' in Form N 134. In case there is advance collection of water supply charges, the Advance Collection A/c shall be credited. Trigger Point: At the end of the month for all collections made during the month Source Document: Form N-134, Water Supply-Summary statement of year- wise head-wise collection of Water Taxes' in Form N 134 Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account NOTICE FEES, WARRANT FEES AND OTHER FEES 4 Recording of demand raised Debit 431-30-03 Receivables for Fees & User Charges-Water Supply (Current Year) Credit 140-40-08 To Other Fees-Notice Fee Credit 140-40-09 To Other Fees-Warrant Fee Explanation: A Summary Statement of Notice Fee, Warrant Fee and Other Fees in Form N-113, shall be prepared on a monthly basis and sent to the Accounts Department. The above entry shall be passed to record the demand raised Trigger Point: At the end of the Month for demand of all notice fee and warrant fee, raised during the month Source Document: Demand Register (Form N-109), Summary Statement of Notice Fee, Warrant Fee and Other Fees in Form N-113 Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 5 Recording of refunds/ remissions payable Debit 140-90-03 Fees Remission & Refund-Water Fees Debit 280-60-01 Prior Period Expenses Refund of Other Revenues- Fees and User Charges Credit 350-41-06 To Advance collection of revenue - Fees and User charges Explanation: A Summary Statement of Water Supply - Summary Statement of Refunds/Remissions Form N-135, shall be prepared on a monthly basis by the water supply department. Refunds/Remissions pertaining to prior period should be identified separately from the current period based on which the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: At the end of the month for all refunds and remissions during the month Source Document: Demand Register in Form N 109 , Summary Statement of Water Supply - Summary Statement of Refunds/Remissions Form N-135, Register of Refunds, Remissions and Write-offs N-114 Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 6 Adjustment of Advance collection of revenue-Fees and User charges Debit 350-41-06 Advance collection of revenue Water Supply Income Credit 431-30-03 To Receivables for Fees & User Charges -Water Supply (Current Year) Explanation: At the time of accrual of income, the above entry shall be passed, to adjust the Advance collection of revenue for receivables for Fees and User charges of water supply of the current year. Trigger Point: At the time of Accrual of income and subsequent adjustment for Advance collection of revenue made during the preceding month/ year. Source Document: Water Supply - Summary Statement of Demand Raised on Assessment (N-133), Water Supply - Summary Statement of Year-wise, Head- wise Collection of Water Taxes(N-134) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 7 Recovery of water supply income through legal proceedings The transaction entries for the recovery of water supply through legal proceedings shall be similar to those passed for recovery of Property Tax through legal proceedings INCOMES TO BE ACCOUNTED ON ACTUAL RECEIPT BASIS The following income, relating to the water supply, shall be accounted for on cash basis (cid:183) Connection Charges for Water Supply; (cid:183) Water Tanker Charges; (cid:183) Road Damage Recovery Charges; and (cid:183) Penalties levied in respect of Water Supply related matters. 8 Recording of collection of income Debit 450-21-01 Bank Account Credit 140-40-06 To Other Fees - Connection Charges Credit 140-50-15 To User Charges - Water Tanker Credit 140-70-04 To Service/ Administrative Charges - Road Damage Recovery Credit 140-20-03 To Penalties & Fines-West Bengal Municipal Act, 1993 Explanation: On the basis of the Summary of Daily Collection (Form 16 and 17) received from the various Collection Offices and Collection Centers, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: End of the day on Receipt of Daily Collection Statement Source Document: Daily collection Challan as Per form 16, Summary of daily collection (N 105) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 9 Recording of Deposits towards water related issues Debit 450-21-01 Bank Account Credit 340-20-01 To Deposit Revenues -Water Connection Explanation: The Water Supply Department may receive Deposits towards Water Connection, Water Meter, Water Tankers, etc. The Collection Offices and Collection Centers authorized to collect Water Deposits shall prepare a Summary of Daily collection (Form N 105) and send it to the Water Supply Department and the Accounts Department. The Accounts Department shall pass the above entry on the basis of the Summary of Daily Collection (Form N 105) received from the various Collection Offices and Collection Centers Trigger Point: End of the day on Receipt of Daily Collection Statement Source Document: Daily Collection Challan (Form 16), Summary of Daily Collection (Form N 105), Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 10 Recording of adjustment of Deposits Debit 340-20-01 Deposit Revenues -Water Connection Credit 431-30-03 To Receivables for Fees & User Charges-Water Supply (Current year) Explanation: On the basis of the instruction received from Water Supply Department for adjustment of deposit against receivables, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: Receipt of instruction from the Water Supply Department Source Document: Instruction from Department, Deposit register(Form N 69) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 11 Recording of refund of Deposit Debit 340-20-01 Deposit Revenues-Water connection Credit 450-21-01 To Bank Account Explanation: The Deposits shall be refunded as per the rules prescribed in this regard. A Payment Order (Form 54) shall be prepared by the Water Supply Department for the Deposits to be refunded. On refund of Water Deposit, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Trigger Point: End of the day on Receipt of Daily Collection Statement and drawing of cheque Source Document: Bank Payment Voucher (Form N 98) , Deposit register(Form N 69) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 12 Recording of Income, in respect of Lapsed Deposits Debit 340-20-01 Deposit Revenues - Water Connection Credit 180-11-03 To Lapsed Deposit-Water Supply Deposits Explanation: Deposits not claimed within the period as laid down by the ULB, from the date they are due for payment, shall be lapsed and shall not be repayable to the party and when the right for claiming refund of Deposit has also expired. Also the Deposits forfeited for the unsuccessful performance of the work, when they are required to be forfeited as per the contract shall not be repayable to the party. At the end of the accounting year, the Water Supply Department shall prepare a list of such lapsed Deposits. To recognize the income, on obtaining the approval of the Authorized Officer, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Trigger Point: Receipt of information from the Water Supply Department, with regard to Deposits to be forfeited Source Document: Letter from the Mayor/Chairperson for the forfeiture of the Deposit, statement received from the Water Supply Department, showing Deposits to be forfeited, Deposit Register(Form N69) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.4 Assigned Revenues
text: 1. INTRODUCTION 1.1 Assigned revenues are the revenues, which is passed on by the Central/State/ Government Agencies to the ULB like Entertainment Tax. The assigned revenues shall be accounted during the year only upon actual collection. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Recording of receipt of assigned revenues Debit 450-21-01 Bank Account Credit 120-10-01 To Taxes and Duties Collected by Others - Entertainment Taxes Explanation: Details of Assigned revenues received shall be entered in the Receipt Register {Form N 101}maintained at the Accounts Department and Deposited into the Main Bank Account and also a Receipt (Form 10) shall be issued to the State Government. To record the receipt of assigned revenues, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Trigger Point: At the time of the receipt of the assigned revenues Source Document: Government order showing the amount of the Grant, Receipt Register, Receipt Register {Form N 101} , Form 17 (Daily Collection Challan book for receipts other than Taxes) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.5 Rental Fees and Other Income
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 Rental fees and other income consist of Rentals from Municipal Properties, License Fees and User charges for facilities fees, sale and hire charges. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Accounting for income to be recorded on actual receipt basis Debit 450-21-01 Bank Account Credit 140-10-01 To Registration of Carts Credit 140-12-01 To Transit Permit Credit 140-13-01 To Fees for Copying Credit 140-14-01 To Development fees Credit 140-15-01 To Encroachment fees Credit 140-40-02 To Tuition Fees Explanation: Based on Daily Collection Challan (Form 16) and Summary of daily collection (Form N 105) received from various Collecting Offices/Centers, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Trigger Point: End of the day on Receipt of Daily Collection Statement Source Document: Daily Collection Challan (Form 16) and Summary of daily collection (Form N 105) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 2 Recording of Deposits In respect of certain Incomes (including incomes accounted for on Accrual basis), a Deposit is taken at the time of receiving the application for providing the services/goods, e.g., Rent Deposit, etc. The accounting for the different transactions in respect of Deposits received is been discussed below, using the example of Rent Deposit. Debit 450-21-01 Bank Account Credit 340-20-03 To Deposits Revenues-Rental Deposits Explanation: On receipt of Deposit, the same shall be treated as a Liability. Trigger Point: On the receipt of the rent deposit Source Document: Daily Collection Challan (Form 16), Summary of daily collection (Form N 105) , Deposit register(Form N 69) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 3 Recording of adjustment of Deposits Debit 340-20-03 Deposit Revenues-Rental Deposits Credit 130-10-01 To Rental Income from-Municipal properties - Market Explanation: The above entry will be passed when ULBs adjust the Deposits received against the cost of the services rendered by it, to the customer. On adjustment of Deposits, the respective departments shall send the details of Deposits adjusted in the Summary Statement of Deposits Adjusted (Form N-108) to the Accounts Department on a monthly basis Trigger Point: At the end of the month, for all Deposits adjusted during the month Source Document: Summary Statement of Deposits Adjusted (Form N-108) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 4 Recording of refund of Deposits Debit 340-20-03 Deposit Revenues-Rental Deposits Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The Deposits shall be refunded when an application is made for it as per the rules prescribed for the same. A Payment Order (Form N 106) shall be prepared by the respective departments for the refund of the Deposits. Trigger Point: At the time of refund of the Deposit Source Document: Summary statement of Deposits Adjusted (Form N-108), Payment Voucher (Form N-98), Application for the refund of the Deposit Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 5 Recording of income in case of lapsed Deposit Debit 340-20-03 Deposit Revenues-Rent Credit 180-11-04 To Lapsed Deposits-Rental Deposits Explanation: Deposits not claimed within the period as laid down by the ULB, from the date they are due for payment and when the right for claiming refund of Deposit has also expired, shall be lapsed and shall not be repayable to the party. Trigger Point: At the end of the month, for all Deposits adjusted during the month Source Document: Summary statement of Deposits Adjusted(Form N-108) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 6 Accounting for demand raised for rental incomes Debit 431-40-01 Receivables for Rental Income Credit 130-10-01 To Rent from Markets Credit 130-10-02 To Rent from Shopping Complexes Explanation: The demand raised for rental incomes shall be recognized as an income for the ULB and correspondingly as a current Asset. Accordingly, based on the (Form N-137) Rental fees & other-income-Summary Statement of Demand raised on assessment received from the respective departments, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry (Illustration for Markets and Shopping Complexes). The above entry shall be passed for the current demand, excluding arrears if any. Trigger Point: At the time of the Accrual i.e. at the start of the month, quarter, half-year or year, on the basis of agreement Source Document: Form N-137, Rental fees & other-income-Summary Statement of Demand raised on assessment Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 7 Collection of Rental income Debit 450-21-01 Bank Account Credit 431-80-06 To Receivable Control Account-Rent Explanation: To record the income based on the Summary of Daily Collections (Form N 105) received from the various Collection Offices and Collection Centers, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Summary of Daily Collection (Form N 105) does not provide the break-up of the collections into arrears collected, collections received in Advance and collection made in respect of the current year's demand. Hence, the total amount collected is credited to "Receivable Control Account" which shall be segregated on a monthly basis. Trigger Point: On receipt of money and on the basis of Summary of daily collection statement Source Document: Summary statement of Daily Collection (Form N-105) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 8 Recording of break up of collections Debit 431-80-06 Receivable Control Account-Rent Credit 431-40-01 To Receivables for Rental Income Credit 350-41-02 To Advance Collection of Revenues-Rent Explanation: A Summary Statement of Year-wise/ Head-wise Collection of various other incomes in Form N-129 (Rental Fees & Other income-Summary statement of year wise head wise collection of other income), shall be prepared on a monthly basis by the respective departments and sent to the Accounts Department to record the details of collection. To record the break-up of the collections made in respect of Incomes accounted for on Accrual basis, into current and arrears recovery and to record any income collected in Advance, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: At the end of month for all collections made during the month Source Document: Rental Fees & Other income-Summary statement of year wise head wise collection of other income( Form N-129) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account NOTICE FEES, WARRANT FEE AND OTHER FEES 1 Recording of demand raised A Summary Statement of Notice Fee, Warrant Fee and Other Fees Charged in Form N-113, Summary Statement of Notice Fees, Warrant Fees, and Other Fees, shall be prepared on a monthly basis and sent to the Accounts Department. For recording the demand raised in respect of Notice Fee, Warrant Fee and Other Fees charged for rent not received, on the basis of the Summary Statement of Notice Fee, Warrant Fee and Other Fees Charged received from the respective departments, the Accounts Department shall pass the same entry as explained in Section 5.1- Property Tax. 2 Recording of refunds/remissions payable A Summary Statement of Refunds and Remissions in Form N-130 (Rentals, Fees & Other Income - Summary Statement of Refunds) shall be prepared on a monthly basis. Refunds / Remissions pertaining to prior period shall be identified separately from the current period. The entry shall be passed as described in Section 5.1 - Property Tax. 3 Adjustment of incomes received in Advance Debit 350-41-02 Advance Collection of Revenues-Rent Credit 431-40-01 To Receivables from Other Sources-Rent Explanation: The above entry shall be passed to adjust the Advance collection of Revenues against the receivables of the current year Trigger Point: At the time of accrual of income, for advance collection of income Source Document: Form N-109, Demand Register Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 4 Provision for doubtful receivables The entry for creating of provision, writing off of the excess provision, recording of write offs, subsequent collection of write offs shall be similar to as described in entries of Section 5.1 - Property Tax. 5 Recovery of income through legal proceedings The transaction entry for recovery of income through legal proceedings will be similar to as described in Section 5.1 - Property Tax. LICENSE FEES License fees shall be recognized on actual receipt of cash 1 Recording of income collections Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 140-11-01 To Licensing Fees for D&O Credit 140-11-02 To Licensing Fees for Hawkers Credit 140-11-03 To Licensing Fees for Shops Credit 140-11-04 To Licensing Fees for Baazars Explanation: The above entry shall be passed on the actual collection of licensee fees. Trigger Point: At the time of the actual collection of the license fees. Source Document: Summary of daily collection(Form N-105) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.6 Employee Related Transactions
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 Salary payments form the bulk of all payments of the ULB. Salary is paid on the 1st of the next month for the previous month. 1.2 Accounting for salary can be divided into two parts viz. Payroll Accounting and Accounting for Retirement Benefits. In the case of payroll accounting, the entries are first passed when the salary becomes due, secondly when the salary is paid, thirdly when loans, advances or other claims are given to the employees and finally when there is repayment by the employees. In the case of payment for retirement benefits such as gratuity and pension, the same is accounted for on actual payment basis. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Recording of salary, allowances and corresponding liability Debit 210-10-01 Establishment Expenses- Basic Salary Debit 210-10-02 Establishment Expenses- Dearness Allowance Debit 210-10-03 Establishment Expenses-Additional Dearness Allowance Debit 210-10-04 Establishment Expenses- House Rent Allowance Debit 210-10-05 Establishment Expenses- Medical Allowance Debit 210-20-05 Benefits and Allowances-Education Allowance Debit 210-30-04 Pension Contribution for deputation staff Debit 210-40-03 Leave Salary contribution for deputation staff Credit 350-11-01 To Employees Liabilities - Gross Salary Payable Explanation: The above entry shall be passed for recognising the salary Expenses under various heads and the Liability to the employees in respect of gross salaries. The entries are made once the final amount is calculated by the Accounts Department upon receipt of details from the Establishment Department But in the case of conservancy staffs, the details are received from the Conservancy Department Trigger Point: When salary become due, on the last date of the month Source Document: Form - 89 Establishment Check Register, Form 45 Bill for pay and fixed allowances Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger 2 Recording of Statutory and other deductions and recoveries from salaries Debit 350-11-01 Employees Liabilities-Gross Salary Payable Credit 350-20-05 To Recoveries Payable-TDS from Employees Credit 350-20-03 To Recoveries Payable- Profession Tax Credit 311-71-01 To Provident Fund (for own employees) Credit 350-20-01 To Recoveries payable-Provident Fund for Employees on Deputation Credit 460-10-01 To Loans and Advances to employees-House Building Advance Credit 460-10-01 To Loans and Advances to employees-Festival Advance Credit 431-40-04 To Receivables from other sources-Interest due on Loans given to employees Credit 460-20-01 To Employee Provident Fund Loans-Housing Loans Credit 350-11-02 To Employee Liabilities-Net Salaries Payable Explanation: For accounting liabilities towards amounts payable to different authorities in respect of statutory deductions and dues towards settlement of obligations on behalf of employees (e.g. housing loan, contribution to various societies, loan recovery for external agencies, group insurance premium, investment by employees, etc.), recovery of instalments of loans or interest levied on loan and/or advances from employees, etc., the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: When salary become due, on the last date of the month Source Document: Form N 67 Register of Advance , Form N 150 Register of Interest on Loans to employees, Form N 151 Unpaid Salary Register Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger 3 Payment of Salaries by Cash (i) On withdrawal of cash Debit 450-10-01 Cash Account Credit 450-21-01 To Bank Account Explanation: For payment of salaries, it is generally paid in cash. The above entry will be passed for withdrawal of cash from Bank. Trigger Point: When cash is withdrawn from Bank Source Document: Cheque, Form N 107(Cheque issue Register) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger (ii) On Payment of net salary by Cash Debit 350-11-02 Employee Liabilities-Net Salaries Payable Credit 450-10-01 To Cash Account Explanation: When cash is paid to the staff, the above entry shall be passed Trigger Point: At the time of payment of cash Source Document: Cash payment voucher(N 98) , Form 82 Register of disbursement of cash drawn on cheques in favour of Chairman or EO or some other officer Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger 4 Recording of payment of Salaries through Bank Debit 350-11-02 Employee Liabilities-Net Salaries Payable Credit 450-21-01 To Bank Account Explanation: For accounting of payment of salary by cheque or direct Credit of the salary to employee Bank account, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: At the time of Payment/Crediting of Salary to Employee account Source Document: Form 45 ( Bill for pay and fixed allowances), Consolidated pay bill and acquittance roll of the permanent/temporary establishment (N 148) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger 5 Recording of payment of Statutory Liabilities made from salaries Debit 350-20-05 Recoveries Payable-TDS from Employees Debit 350-20-03 Recoveries Payable- Profession Tax Credit 450-21-01 To Bank Account Explanation: For discharging liabilities towards statutory deductions (e.g., Income Tax, Profession Tax, etc.), on payment, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: At the time of making payment Source Document: Register of bills for payment(Form N 79) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger 6 Recording of Liabilities for Unpaid Salary (Cheques not encashed) Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 350-11-03 To Employee Liabilities-Unpaid Salaries Payable Explanation: In case cheques issued to the employees have not been encashed for six months, such cheques shall be cancelled. On cancellation of cheques, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Trigger Point: At the end of six months from issue of cheques Source Document: Bank Reconciliation Statements(N 126), Unpaid Salary Register(N 151) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger 7 Recording of Payment of Unpaid Salaries Debit 350-11-03 Employee Liabilities-Unpaid Salaries Payable Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Credit 450-10-01 To Cash A/c Explanation: On subsequent payment of unpaid salaries through Bank or Cash, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: At the time of Payment of Unpaid Salaries Source Document: Unpaid Salary Register (N 151) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger 8 Recording of Loans and Advances given to Employees Debit 460-10-01 Loans and Advances to Employees-House Building Advance Debit 460-10-02 Loans and Advances to Employees-Conveyance Advance Debit 460-10-03 Loans and Advances to Employees-Computer Advance Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: ULB may give loans (interest bearing or interest free) and advances (temporary, standing, etc.) to the employees. On payment of loans and advances by the ULB, the Register of Employee Loans / Advances (N 149) shall be updated by the Accounts Department. These Registers shall be maintained Department-wise. For recording the payment of loans and advances to employees, the Accounts Department shall pass the following entries. (Illustration above mentions loans for House building, conveyance and computer) Trigger Point: At the time of giving Advance to employees Source Document: Register of Employee Loans / Advances (N 149), Application for Advances, Approval from Mayor/Chairperson Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger 9 Recognising interest on loans and advances granted to the employees at the period-end Debit 431-40-05 Receivable from Other Sources-Interest due from Advances to Employees Credit 171-20-01 To Interest on Loans and advances to Employees - House Building Advance Credit 171-20-02 To Interest on Loans and advances to Employees - Conveyance Advance Credit 171-20-03 To Interest on Loans and advances to Employees - Computer Advance Explanation: As per the terms and conditions of the loan agreement, interest is levied on the outstanding balance of loans. The amount of interest accrued shall be communicated by the concerned Department to the Accounts Department at the period-ends, for which an entry shall be made in the Register of Interest on Loans to Employees (Form N-150). On the basis of intimation received, the Accounts Department shall pass the above Entry. Trigger Point: At the time of the Accrual of the interest Source Document: Calculation sheet of amount of interest, Form N-150 Register of Interest on Loans to Employees Documents Updated: Journal Book, Ledger 10 Recording of payment of Provident Fund Liability of own employees Debit 311-71-01 Provident Fund (for own employees) Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The Accounts Department shall transfer the amount deducted as contribution from Main Bank Account to the Provident Fund Bank Account maintained with the Treasury and pass the above entry. Trigger Point: At the time of transfer of the amount from general Bank Account to the designated Provident Fund Bank Account Source Document: Consolidated pay bill and acquittance roll of the permanent/temporary establishment (N 148), Establishment Check Register (Form 89) Records Updated: Cash book, Ledger 11 Payment of Provident Fund Contribution in respect of employees on deputation Debit 350-20-01 Recoveries payable - Provident Fund for Employees on Deputation Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: Provident Fund Contribution deducted on behalf of State Government or other organizations/authorities from the salary of employees on deputation to the ULB shall be Deposited with the relevant authority. On payment, the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry. Trigger Point: At the time of making payment to the relevant authority Source Document: Form 89 Establishment Check Register Records Updated: Cash book, Ledger Account RETIREMENT BENEFITS Retirement benefits for the employees of the ULB usually consist of Pension and Gratuity. These shall be accounted on cash basis. 12 On payment of pension at the end of the month Debit 210-30-01 Pension Debit 210-30-02 Family Pension Debit 210-30-04 Pension-Pension Contribution Employees on Deputation Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The entry shall be passed, at the end of the month for actual payment of pension. Trigger Point: At the time of payment Source Document: Form -89 Establishment Check Register, N-153 Pension Register, Cheque Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 13 At the time of Payment of gratuity Debit 210-40-04 Death cum Retirement Gratuity Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The above entry shall be passed as and when the payment for the gratuity is made Trigger Point: At the time of making of Payment Source Document: Establishment check Register Form-89, Service book, Cheque Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.7 Health and Sanitation
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 The health and sanitation activities are basically concerned with operations of the hospitals, dispensaries & maternity homes and in monitoring sanitation and other public health related activities, within the limits of the ULB. 1.2 The main sources of income with regard to health and sanitation are as follows: i. Fees and User Charges a. Fees from Medical Institutions b. Registration Fees for Patients c. Recovery of Cost of Medicines d. Examination Charges [X -Rays, Tests, Ultra-Sound] e. Ambulance Charges f. Funeral/Hearse Van Services g. Disposal Charges (Solid Waste) ii. Sale & Hire Charges a. Sale of Garbage/Manure 1.3 The main sources of Expenditure with regard to health and sanitation are as follows i. Establishment Expenses ii. Administrative Expenses iii. Repair and Maintenance iv. Solid Waste Management related Expenses, such as City Cleaning and Sweeping, Garbage clearance v. Hospital and Dispensaries related Expenses such as Medical related Expenses, Testing and Inspection fees, Consumption of Medical stores, etc. 2. ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Recording of Income from Hospital and Dispensaries Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 140-10-03 To Fees and User Charges - Registration of patients Credit 140-50-01 To User Charges-Sale of Medicines Credit 140-50-02 To User Charges-Examination Charges in Hospitals Credit 140-50-03 To User Charges-Ambulance Charges Explanation: On the basis of the Summary of Daily Collection (Form N 105) Received from the various entities i.e. units, Ward Offices, Health Department, etc., the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: At the time of receipt of the Daily collection statement Source Document: Daily Collection Challan (Form-16) and Summary of Daily Collection (Form N105) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 2 Recording of income from Sanitation activities Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 140-50-05 To User Charges-Garbage Collection Fees Credit 140-50-07 To User Charges-Septic Tank Clearance Charges Credit 140-50-08 To User Charges-Sewerage Clearance Charges Explanation: On the basis of the Summary of Daily Collection (Form N 105) received from the various entities, i.e., Units, Ward Offices, Health Department, etc., the Accounts Department shall pass the above entry Trigger Point: At the time of receipt of the Daily collection statement Source Document: Daily Collection Challan (Form 16), Summary of Daily Collection (Form N 105) Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account 3. At the period-end, the Health Department shall ensure that all the bills received in respect of Expenses incurred before the last date of the accounting period, are processed and forwarded to the Accounts Department for accounting by 31 May of the next financial year or before the finalisation of accounts, whichever is earlier.

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.8 Other Revenue Expenditure
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section contains the transaction entries relating to revenue expenditure other than employees related Expenses, Public Works and Stores. The expenditures discussed in this section are those in respect of which the benefits expire in a short period of time, i.e., less than one year. 1.2 The transaction entries discussed in this section illustratively depict entries for Administrative Expenses, Programme Expenses, Miscellaneous Expenses, Operation and Maintenance and other Revenue Expenses. 2. ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Recording of expenditure Debit 220-12-01 Communication Expenses-Telephone Expenses Debit 220-21-02 Printing Debit 220-21-03 Stationary Debit 230-10-01 Power and Fuel-Electricity Expenses Debit 230-50-01 Repair and Maintenance-Roads and Pavements Debit 230-50-03 Repair and Maintenance-Cars Credit 350-10-03 To Expenses Payable Explanation: The instant expenditure is approved, it has to be accrued as a liability and expenses payable booked. Trigger Point: On receipt of approved bill/ invoice from the concerned department/ official Source Document: Bills of the Expenses, Approval for the Expenses Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account, 2 Recording payment of expenditure Debit 350-10-03 Expenses Payable Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The accrued bill, once it is paid will be recognised as above. Trigger Point: At the time of making payment Source Document: Bills of the Expenses, Approval for the Expenses, Register of bills for payment (N 79) Records Updated: Bank Book, Ledger Account 3 Expenditures involving an element of pre-payment (i) On making of the payment for the expenditure Debit 440-20-01 Pre-paid Expenses-Administration- Rent, Rates and Taxes Debit 350-10-03 Expenses Payable Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The above entry shall be passed in case pre-payment is being made. The amount of current year's liability shall be debited to Expenses Payable A/c and the balance recognised as current Asset. The example shown above relates to Rent, Rates and Taxes Trigger Point: At the time of making Payment of the expenses already booked on accrual basis Source Document: Rent Agreement, Rent Bills, Demand and collection Register of rents-Form 37 Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account (ii) Recording of rent Expenses in the next year Debit 350-10-03 Expenses Payable Credit 440-20-01 To Pre-paid Expenses administration Rent, Rates and Taxes Explanation: The above entry shall be passed at the start of the financial year. To book the expenditure relating to the year. Trigger Point: At the start of the Financial year Source Document: Balance Sheet as at start of the year, Vouchers for the Pre- paid Expenses , Register of bills for payment (N-79) Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger 4 Recording of expenditure for which no bills are received (i) Recording of expenditure Debit 220-10-01 Rent, Rates and Taxes-Office Building rent Debit 220-12-01 Communication Expenses-Telephone Expenses Debit 230-10-01 Power and Fuel-Electricity Expenses Credit 360-10-01 To Provision for Expenses-Rent, Rates and Taxes Credit 360-10-03 To Provision for Expenses-Communication Expenses Credit 360-10-13 To Provision for Expenses-Power and Fuel Explanation: The above entry shall be passed for those Expenses incurred, but for which no bills have been received thus on the basis of the estimated amount. Trigger Point: At the Year end/Cut-off Date Source Document: Form N 79, Register of bills for payment Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger (ii) Payment of the accrued expenditure Debit 360-10-01 Provision for Expenses-Rent, Rates and Taxes Debit 360-10-03 Provision for Expenses-Communication Expenses Debit 360-10-13 Provision for Expenses Power and Fuel Credit 350-10-03 To Expenses Payable Explanation: The above entry shall be passed in the next accounting year, upon receipt of bill Trigger Point: At the start of the next Financial year after receipt of bill Source Document: Form N 79, Register of bills for payment Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account 5 Payment against Permanent Advance (i) Recording of Grant of Permanent Advance Debit 460-50-01 Advance to Others-Permanent Advance Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The permanent Advance is made to executive officer or other person for making certain specified payments. Trigger Point: At the Time of making Payment for the Permanent Advance Source Document: Form N 49 Register of permanent advance Account, Approval from Mayor/Chairperson Records Updated: Cash Book, Ledger Account (ii) Recording of revenue expenditure incurred out of permanent Advance Debit 220-20-01 Books and Periodicals-Magazines Debit 220-30-05 Travelling and Conveyance- Petrol and Diesel Credit 460-50-01 To Advance to Others-Permanent Advance Explanation: Expenditure in respect of which Permanent Advance has been provided shall be initially incurred against it. The concerned Person/ Department shall prepare a Payment order in Form no-54. The Accounts Department shall make entry on the basis of the payment order. Trigger Point: Submission of Payment order Source Document: Form N 106 , Payment order Records Updated: Journal Book, Ledger Account

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.9 Public Works
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section contains the illustrative accounting entries for Public Works transactions undertaken by the ULBs. These would generally include construction/ expansion/ major modification of buildings, development of land, construction of roads, construction of water works, construction of drainage systems or other public utilities. Repairs and maintenance of these would also constitute Public Works. Apart from these, any non-civil contracts (e.g., for furniture) undertaken by the ULB through the Public Works Department would also be covered here. This section also covers the accounting treatment for 'Deposit works' executed by the ULB on behalf of the Government or Government Departments. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES EARNEST MONEY AND SECURITY DEPOSIT 1 Sale of Tender Forms and receipt of Earnest Money Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/C Credit 150-11-01 To Sale of tender forms Credit 340-10-01 To Earnest Money Deposit Explanation: The first step in the contracting process is issue of tender forms to the contractor. On purchase of tender the contractors pay a prescribed fee and Earnest Money Deposit which is accounted as above. In case earnest money is received in respect of any contract to be executed under any specific Grant, the respective Bank Account should be used. In case, the Earnest Money Deposit is in the form of collateral Deposits such as NSC, then no entry shall be passed for the same. Trigger Point: Receipt of Fees from sale of documents, earnest money Source Document: Receipt of money, Summary of Daily Collection (Form N 105) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 2 Refund of Earnest Money to unsuccessful bidders Debit 340-10-01 Earnest Money Deposit Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: Once the tender is opened and evaluated a list of unsuccessful bidders are prepared. They are refunded their earnest money and this entry is passed to account for the same. In case earnest money is received in respect of any contract to be executed under any specific Grant, the respective Bank Account should be used. Trigger Point: Refund of Earnest money Source Document: Deposit Register (Form N 69) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 3 Conversion of Earnest Money Deposit into Security Deposit Debit 340-10-01 Earnest Money Deposit Credit 340-10-02 To Security Deposit Explanation: Once the tender is opened and evaluated, a particular bidder will be selected and his earnest money will have to be converted as Security Deposit Trigger Point: Selection of a contractor and award of contract Source Document: Summary Sheet of Contractors and Tender Register, Form N 108 (Summary Statement of Deposits adjusted) Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 4 Receipt of Security Deposit Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 340-10-02 To Security Deposit Explanation: The selected contractor may be required to deposit some more money as Security Deposit once the contract is awarded. In case Security Deposit is received in respect of any contract to be executed under any specific Grant, the respective Bank Account should be used. Trigger Point: Selection of a contractor and award of contract Source Document: Deposit Register(Form N 69) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger ADVANCES TO CONTRACTORS 5 Approval of Advance to Contractors Debit 460-40-01 Advance for Public Works Credit 350-10-07 To Contractors Advance Control Account Credit 350-20-06 To TDS Payable Explanation: In some cases Mobilisation Advance is given to the contractor. In such case, once the Advance is approved, it has to be routed through the Contractor Control Account and Liability to pay TDS created as well Trigger Point: Approval of Advance to contractor Source Document: Approval by competent authority, Contract agreement Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 6 Payment of Advance to Contractors Debit 350-10-07 Contractors Advance Control Account Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: In some cases Mobilisation Advance is given to the contractor. In such case, once the Advance is approved, it has to be routed through the Contractor Control Account and Liability to pay TDS created as well. This entry is for payment of the Advance to the contractor through the control Account. In case Advance to be given in respect of any contract to be executed under any specific Grant, the respective Bank Account should be used. TDS will be paid on the due date by debiting the TDS payable A/c. Trigger Point: Payment of Advance to contractor Source Document: Approval by competent authority, Register of Advance(Form N 67) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 7 When Material is issued to the contractor Debit 460-40-03 Material issued to the contractors Credit 430-10-01 To Central Stores Explanation: When ULBs issue materials to the contractor, the above entry will be passed e.g. from central stores. This material may be cement, steel etc. and would be used during the course of construction under the contract. Trigger Point: Issue of materials Source Document: Stores Issue Note, Contract Agreement, Material Requisition cum Issue Note(Form N 145) Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger CONTRACTOR'S PAYMENT 8 Passing of Contractor's Bill Debit 412-10-01 Capital Work-In-Progress- Buildings Credit 340-10-02 To Security Deposit Credit 460-40-01 To Advance for Public Works Credit 350-20-05 To TDS Payable Credit 350-20-04 To Works contract Tax payable Credit 460-40-03 To Material issued to the contractors Credit 350-10-02 To Contractor Payable A/c Explanation: The Bill is prepared by the Engineering Department and sent to Accounts for payment after approval by the Chief Engineer. The above entry is passed to recognise the Liability e.g. for buildings created out of specific Grants. The amount due to the contractor for all expenditure related to construction for the particular project will be credited to him by the above entry. Details of Bills as well as Contract No. / reference will also be recorded to exercise proper control over contractor balances. In case the work to be executed is out of specific Grant, the same accounts shall be used. Trigger Point: Measurement of the work done by the contractor and approval of the Bill by the Chief Engineer Source Document: Measurement Book and Running Bill received from the Engineering Department, Contract agreement Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 9 Payment to Contractors Debit 350-10-02 Contractor Payable A/c Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The above entry will be passed at time of release of payment to the contractor. Trigger Point: Approval of Running Bill of the contractor by Chairman/Mayor and preparation of the cheque Source Document: Approved running Bill of the contractor and cheque, Register of bills for payment (N 79) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 10 Deduction from bills of Contractors Debit 350-10-02 Contractor Payable A/c Credit 180-80-11 To Penalties/ damages from contractors Explanation: For deductions made from contractor's bills, which is in the nature of penalty or liquidated damages levied on the contractor for delay in completion of construction or sub-standard construction or for any other reason. Trigger Point: Approval of Running Bill of the contractor with indication for deduction of penalties or damages by Chairman/Mayor Source Document: Approved running Bill of the contractor, Contract Agreement Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 11 Adjustment of recoveries against Security Deposit Debit 340-10-02 Security Deposit Credit 180-80-13 To Other Income - Recoveries from contractors Explanation: After the contract is completed to the satisfaction of the Municipal Engineers, Security Deposit is returned to the contractor as per the terms of the contract. It may so happen that the contractor does not perform as per the contract terms and so part of the Deposit is forfeited or there are certain recoveries that need to be done. In such cases the part of the Deposit which is not refunded to the contractor as per decision of the authorised official will be treated as income under the Account head Other Income. Trigger Point: When the security Deposited is forfeited as per the decision of the authorised official. Source Document: Approval of the appropriate authority, N 69 Deposit Register, N108 Summary statement of deposits adjusted Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 12 Refund of Deposit to Contractors Debit 340-10-02 Security Deposit Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: After the contract is completed to the satisfaction of the Municipal Engineers, Security Deposit is returned to the contractor as per the terms of the contract. The same would be accounted for in the manner shown above. In case earnest money/security Deposit is received in respect of any contract to be executed under any specific Grant, the respective Bank Account should be used. Trigger Point: Payment of Security Deposit to Contractor Source Document: Approval by the appropriate authority, Deposit ledger(Form N 69), Summary statement of deposits adjusted(Form N 108) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 13 Recognition of Lapsed Deposit of Contractors as income Debit 340-10-02 Security Deposit Debit 340-10-01 Earnest Money Deposit Credit 180-11-01 To Lapsed Deposits - Contractors Explanation: Security Deposit or Earnest Money Deposit is considered as revenue at the time when intimation is made by the Engineering Department, with the approval of the Mayor/ Chairperson, about the unsuccessful performance of the work and as per the contract, the money is forfeited and when the right for claiming refund of Deposit has also expired. Trigger Point: Unsuccessful performance of the work, right for claiming refund of Deposit has also expired Source Document: Approval of the appropriate authority, Deposit ledger(Form N 69), Summary statement of deposits adjusted(Form N 108) Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 14 Payment of Statutory Dues Debit 350-20-05 TDS Payable Debit 350-20-04 Works contract Tax payable Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The amount of TDS and Works Contract Tax deducted from contractors will be remitted on the due dates to the Government. On such payment the above entry is passed. Trigger Point: Remittance of deduction to the Government Account on due date Source Document: Cheque, Ledger Account of TDS Payable and Works Contract Tax payable, running bills of the contractors, Register of bills for payment (N 79) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 15 Adjustment of difference between stores issue rate and recovery rate of materials issued to contractors Debit 460-40-03 Material issued to the contractors Credit 412-10-01 To Capital Work-In-Progress Buildings Explanation: There may be difference between the rate at which materials are issued to the contractor and the rate of recovery of stores for the ULB. The difference has to be either debited or credited to CWIP, to adjust the difference with the capital cost. In case the rate is favourable i.e. the rate of issue is less than the rate of recovery, the difference has to be deducted from capital cost. In case, where the rate of issue is more than the rate of recovery, the difference has to be added to capital cost with an opposite entry. Trigger Point: Issue of materials from stores to the contractor Source Document: Stores Issue Note , Form N 145 Material Requisition cum Issue Note , Contract document with the contractor Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 16 Works executed by PWD Debit 412-10-01 Capital Work-In-Progress Buildings Credit 430-10-01 To Central Stores Explnation: In certain cases, the work is executed by the PWD e.g. Buildings built out of specific Grants and materials are used from stores e.g. Central Stores Trigger Point: Issue of materials from stores/ Bill received from PWD Source Document: Stores Issue Note / Bill of PWD, Form N 142 Work sheet Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger CAPITALISATION 17 Capitalisation of Assets Debit 410-20-01 Fixed Assets- Office Buildings Credit 412-10-01 To Capital Work-In-Progress Buildings Explanation: The amount of Capital WIP e.g. buildings built out of specific Grants, is capitalised under the respective heads e.g. office building. At the time of capitalisation, quantitative details will be entered in the fixed asset system. The above entry will be passed by the Accounts Department. In case, the Asset has been created out of a specific Grant, the specific Grant's CWIP Account shall be credited. Trigger Point: Completion of project/ Asset are ready and put to use/ Issue of completion certificate Source Document: Form N 142 Work sheet , Measurement Book and Running Bill, Completion certificate Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, N 118 Register of Immovable Property 18 Capitalisation of interest on borrowings Debit 412-10-01 Capital Work-In-Progress- Buildings Credit 240-30-03 To Interest and Finance Charges Explanation: Interest on loan borrowed e.g. from KMDA, is capitalised under the respective heads e.g. buildings. Trigger Point: When the work is in progress, the capitalisation shall be done, as and when the interest is accrued. Source Document: Details of borrowings, Register of Loan (Form N 72), Form N 142 Work sheet Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger REPAIRS AND MAINTENANCE 19 Passing of Contractor's Bill for repairs and maintenance Debit 230-50-01 Operation and Maintenance- Roads and pavements Credit 340-10-02 To Security Deposit Credit 460-40-01 To Advance for Public Works Credit 350-20-05 To TDS Payable Credit 350-20-04 To Works contract Tax payable Credit 460-40-03 To Material issued to the contractors Credit 350-10-02 To Contractor Payable A/c Explnation: The Bill is prepared by the Engineering Department and sent to Accounts for payment after approval by the Chief Engineer. The above entry is passed to recognise the Liability e.g. repairs and maintenance on roads and pavements. The amount due to the contractor for all expenditure related to repairs and maintenance for the particular asset will be credited to him by the above entry. Trigger Point: Measurement of the work done by the contractor and approval of the Bill by the Chief Engineer Source Document: Measurement Book and Running Bill received from the Engineering Department Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger Adjustment of difference between stores issue rate and recovery rate of materials issued to contractors Debit 460-40-03 Material issued to the contractors Credit 230-50-01 To Operation and Maintenance-Roads and pavements Explanation: There may be difference between the rate at which materials are issued to the contractor and the rate of recovery of stores for the ULB. The difference has to be either debited or credited to operation and maintenance, e.g. roads and pavements, to adjust the difference. In case the rate is favourable i.e. the rate of issue is less than the rate of recovery, the difference has to be deducted from operation and maintenance cost. In case, where the rate of issue if more than the rate of recovery, the difference has to be added to operation and maintenance with an opposite entry. Trigger Point: Issue of materials from stores to the contractor Source Document: Contract document with the contractor, Form N 145 Material Requisition cum Issue Note, Stores Ledger(Form N 146) Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 21 O & M work executed by PWD Debit 230-30-01 Consumption of stores Central Stores Credit 430-10-01 To Central Stores Explanation: In certain cases, maintenance work is executed by the PWD and materials are used from stores e.g. Central Stores Trigger Point: At the end of the month, for all stores consumed during the month. Source Document: Stores Issue Note / Bill of PWD, Form N 146 Stores Ledger Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger DEPOSIT WORKS 22 Receipt of money for "Deposit works" Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 341-10-01 To Deposit works- Civil works - MPLADS Explanation: ULBs receive money to execute works which is done on behalf of other agencies e.g. received from MPLADS for civil work. The specific Bank Account for MPLADS Fund shall be used. Trigger Point: Receipt of money Source Document: Receipt voucher/Government order, Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 23 Passing of Contractor's Bill w.r.t. Deposit works Debit 470-10-01 Deposit works expenditure Civil works Credit 340-10-02 To Security Deposit Credit 460-40-01 To Advance for Public Works Credit 350-20-05 To TDS Payable Credit 350-20-04 To Works contract Tax payable Credit 460-40-03 To Material issued to the contractors Credit 350-10-02 To Contractor Payable A/c Explanation: The Bill is prepared by the Engineering Department and sent to Accounts for payment after approval by the Chief Engineer. The above entry is passed to recognise the Liability. The amount due to the contractor for all expenditure related to Deposit works e.g. on civil works will be credited to him by the above entry. Details of Bills as well as Contract No/ reference will also be recorded to exercise proper control over contractor's balances. Trigger Point: Measurement of the work done by the contractor and approval of the Bill by the Chief Engineer Source Document: Measurement Book and Running Bill received from the Engineering Department, Work sheet(N 142), Deposit Works Register ( Form N 143) Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger Adjustment of difference between stores issue rate and recovery rate of materials issued to contractors Debit 460-40-03 Material issued to the contractors Credit 470-10-01 To Deposit works expenditure Civil works Explanation: There may be difference between the rate at which materials are issued to the contractor and the rate of recovery of stores for the ULB. The difference has to be either debited or credited to Deposit works e.g. on civil works, to adjust the difference. In case the rate is favourable i.e. the rate of issue is less than the rate of recovery, the difference has to be deducted from Deposit works cost. In case, where the rate of issue if more than the rate of recovery, the difference has to be added to Deposit works with an opposite entry. Trigger Point: Issue of materials from stores to the contractor Source Document: Form N 145, Material requisition cum issue note Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 25 Recording of deductions from contractor's bills Debit 350-10-02 Contractor Payable A/c Credit 341-10-01 To Deposit works Civil works - MPLADS Explanation: For deductions made from contractor's bills, which is in the nature of penalty or liquidated damages levied on the contractor for delay in completion of construction or sub-standard construction or for any other reason Trigger Point: Receipt of intimation from the Executing Department Source Document: Intimation from the Department Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 26 Closure of Deposit works expenditure statement Debit 341-10-01 Deposit works- Civil works - MPLADS Credit 470-10-01 To Deposit works expenditure-Civil works Explanation: On completion of Deposit works e.g. on civil works from MPLADS Funds, the assets under Deposit works becomes ready for use and the balance if any shall be refunded. Thus, it becomes necessary to adjust the cost incurred for Deposit works against the money received. The Account Department shall pass the above for transfer of Deposit Expenses to Deposit Works Account. Trigger Point: Receipt of intimation from the Executing Department Source Document: Intimation from the Department Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 27 Refund of balance of Deposit works money, if any Debit 341-10-01 Deposit works-Civil works - MPLADS Credit 350-40-03 To Refund payable for Deposit works Explanation: The balance amount after utilisation of the amounts for the Deposit works shall be refunded to the Governments/Government Departments by transferring the balance in 'Deposit Works' Account to a Liability Account. On payment of this money, the usual entry of payment shall be passed by debiting the Refund payable for Deposit works Account and crediting the concerned Bank A/c.
