Trigger Point: Receipt of intimation for refund from the Executing Department Source Document: Intimation from the Department, Government order, Work Sheet (Form N 142), Deposit work register(Form N 143) Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.10 Stores
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section contains the illustrative transaction entries for stores related transactions. 1.2 The functions of the Stores, involving procurement, storage, issue, disposal and accounting of materials, may be performed either centrally by Central Stores (referred to as Municipal Stores) or by the Department Stores empowered by the ULB to perform the aforesaid functions for specific departments of the ULB. Generally, the following departments may have their own stores e.g. Public Works, Health - Sanitation, Health - Medical, Water Works, Workshop for vehicles and machinery repairs and Public Lighting Departments. 2. ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Receipt of Earnest Money Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 340-10-01 To Earnest Money Deposit Explanation: The suppliers pay a prescribed fee and Earnest Money Deposit which is accounted as above. Trigger Point: Receipt of Earnest Money Deposit Source Document: Summary of daily collection (Form N 105) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 2 Payment of Advance to Suppliers Debit 460-40-02 Advance for Stores Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: In some cases Advance is given to the supplier. In such case, once the Advance is approved, it has to be routed through the Advance Account. Trigger Point: Payment of Advance to supplier Source Document: Approval by competent authority Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 3 When the materials are purchased from the suppliers Debit 430-10-01 Central Stores Credit 350-10-01 To Suppliers Payable Explanation: When the materials supplied are received by the Stores e.g. central stores and the Bill is approved then the entry shall be passed. In case goods once received, but returned to the supplier, for whatever reason, a reverse entry would be passed Trigger Point: Approval of the Bill by the Mayor/ Chairperson and Store keeper Source Document: Bill of the Supplier and Stores Ledger N 146 Records updated: Journal book, Ledger 4 Adjustment of Advance and Payment to the Supplier Debit 350-10-01 Suppliers Payable Credit 460-40-02 To Advance for Stores Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The entry will be made at the time of payment to the supplier. If there is any Advance given by the supplier then the same shall be adjusted against the payment. Trigger Point: Approval of the Bill by the Mayor/ Chairperson and store keeper Source Document: Bill of the Supplier , Advance register(Form N 67) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 5 When the stores item becomes obsolete Debit 270-40-51 Asset written off - Stores Credit 430-10-01 To Central Stores Explanation: The loss in the value of stores e.g. central stores, can take place due to efflux of time or obsolescence and the stocks are written off. The value is to be shown at lower of net realisable value or book value. In case the net realisable value is less than the book value, the following entry needs to be passed Trigger Point: Decision to write off the stores Source Document: Approval Note by the Mayor/ Chairperson or any other competent officer Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 6 Sale of store items Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 350-90-02 To Sale proceeds Stores Explanation: There may be sale of store items which have become obsolete or otherwise. The following entry needs to be passed in case there is a sale of stores. This will first be credited to a Control Account. Trigger Point: Sale of stores and receipt of money Surce Document: Receipt voucher, Form N 105 Summary of Daily Collection Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 7 Adjustment entry on sale of store items Debit 350-90-02 Sale proceeds-stores Credit 150-12-01 To Sale of stores and spares-Obsolete stores Credit 430-10-01 To Central Stores Explanation: The following entry shall be made from the control Account to the respective accounts. Profit/ Loss on sale of assets will be accounted here. The above entry recognises profit on sale of stores. In case there is loss on sale of stores, 271-10-76 - Loss on sale of assets-Central Stores A/c shall be debited. Trigger Point: Sale of stores and receipt of money Source Document: Stores Ledger Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.11 Investments
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This chapter contains the illustrative transaction entries in relation to investment transactions. .2 Investments shall be classified based on the maturity profile / nature into short-term and long-term. 'Short term' investments are those which are readily realisable, and are intended to be held for not more than twelve months from the date of investment. Investments falling outside the ambit of Short term investments are treated as 'Long Term' investments. There is no difference in accounting entries for short term or long term investments. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Purchase of Investment Debit 420-10-01 Investments-General Fund-Central Government Securities Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Municipal Fund Explanation: The above entry is passed on purchase of investment e.g. of Central Government Securities from Municipal General Fund. The cost of an investment not only includes the purchase price of the investment but also incidental charges like brokerage, fees, stamp duties etc. ULBs may invest a part of the General Fund available to minimise idle Funds and utilise the amount available to earn interest. The investments are in the form as prescribed by Board of Councillors such as fixed deposits/ short term deposits banks, NSCs, shares in approved institutions, debentures, bonds etc. Investment Register is maintained to record details of each investment. In case investments are made from Special Funds or Grants, the specific Fund/ Grant Account will used. Trigger Point: Purchase of Investment Source Document: FDR/ Broker Note/ Debenture certificate/ Bond certificate Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Investment Register, Specific Fund/ Grant Ledger 2 Receipt of Income on Investment made from General Fund Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 170-10-02 To Income from investments-Government Deposits Explanation: Above entry is passed as and when income on investments is received. The same may be in nature of interest on Debentures/Bonds/FDR or dividend on shares. Illustration-Income from investment in Government Deposits. Trigger Point: Receipt of money Source Document: Cheque/ Bank advice Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Investment Register(Form N 157) 3 Receipt of Interest from investments made out of specific Funds/ Grants Debit 450-41-01 Bank A/c-Special Fund Credit 311-50-01 To Earmarked Funds-Sinking Fund Explanation: Above entry is passed as and when income on investments made out of specific funds, e.g. Sinking Fund, or Grants is received. The same may be in nature of interest on Debentures/ Bonds/ Fixed Deposits Trigger Point: Receipt of Money Source Document: Cheque/ Bank advice. Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Investment Register(Form N 157), Specific Grant/ Fund Register 4 Reinvestment of income on Investment made from General Fund Debit 420-10-01 Investments-General Fund-Central Government Securities Credit 170-10-02 To Income from investments-Government Deposits Explanation: Above entry is passed as and when income on investments is not received and reinvested e.g. on Central Government Deposits. Trigger Point: Receipt of statement/ intimation from the Bank/ authority Source Document: Statement/ intimation from Bank Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, Investment Register 5 Reinvestment of income on Investment made from specific Fund/ Grants Debit 421-10-01 Investments-Other Fund-Central Government securities Credit 311-50-01 To Earmarked Funds-Sinking Fund Explanation: Above entry is passed as and when income on investments e.g. on Central Government securities is not received and reinvested. Since Investment in this case has been done out of specific Grants/ Funds e.g. Sinking Fund, the income portion shall be credited to the specific Grant or Fund Trigger Point: Receipt of statement/ intimation from the Bank/ authority Source Document: Statement/ intimation from Bank Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, Investment Register(Form N 157), Special Fund Register(Form N 156) 6 Interest Accrued on Investment made from General Fund Debit 431-40-02 Interest Accrued and due Debit 431-40-03 Interest Accrued but not due Creit 170-10-02 To Income from investments-Government Deposits Explanation: At year-end ULB will account for that part of income which has accrued but not received e.g. from Government Deposits. This will include both interest accrued and due to be received and interest accrued and not due to be received. This will be in line with accrual basis of accounting. E.g. if interest on FDR has been received till December and the Income & Expenditure A/c is prepared in March then interest for three months will be known as interest due and accrued. However in case the interest will be received in say, June, then the interest for the period Jan-Mar will be known as interest accrued but not due. In case interest has accrued from investments made out of specific Funds/ Grants, the concerned Fund/ Grant will be credited instead of income from investments. Trigger Point: End of period/ Financial Year Source Document: Calculation sheet/other details like fixed period in case of time investments , FDR, Bond Certificate Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Investment Register(Form N 157) 7 When Accrued interest of previous year is received in the current year Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 431-40-02 To Interest Accrued and due Credit 431-40-03 To Interest Accrued but not due Explanation: Above entry is passed as & when previous year income is received during the current year. The same may be in nature of interest on debentures/ bonds/ FD or dividend on shares Trigger Point: Receipt of investment income Source Document: Cheque/ Bank advice Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Investment Register(Form N 157) 8 Sale of Investment made out of General Fund Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Debit 271-20-01 Loss on disposal of investments (as applicable) Credit 420-10-01 To Investments- General Fund-Central Government Securities Credit 170-40-01 To Profit on Sale of Investment (as applicable) Explanation: In case investments are sold e.g. Central Government securities, the following entry will be passed. Profit/Loss on sale of investments is recognised through the above entry. Profit/Loss will be calculated by comparing the sale proceeds, net of expenses with the value of investment as appearing in books. Trigger Point: Sale of investment Source Document: Broker Note/ Investment Register (Form N 157)/ Cheque Records undated: Cash Book, Ledger, Investment Register 9 Sale of Investment made out of specific Fund/ Grant Debit 450-41-01 Bank A/c- Special Funds Debit 450-61-01 Bank A/c- Grant Funds Credit 421-10-01 To Investments-Other Fund-Central Government securities Credit 311-50-01 To Earmarked Funds Sinking Fund Explanation: In case investments are sold e.g. Central Government securities, the following entry will be passed. Profit/Loss on sale of investments is recognised through the above entry and credited/ debited to the Specific Fund/ Grant Account e.g. Sinking Fund. Profit/Loss will be calculated by comparing the sale proceeds, net of expenses with the value of investment as appearing in books. Trigger Point: At the time of receipt of the sale proceeds Source Document: Broker Note/Investment Register/Cheque Records undated: Cash Book, Ledger, Investment Register

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.12 Fixed Assets
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section contains the illustrative accounting entries for transactions relating to fixed assets. 1.2 Fixed Assets may be constructed or acquired by or may be gifted or donated to the ULBs either for its own use or for public benefit. The accounting treatment in respect of Fixed Assets constructed by the ULBs has been described in the section on Public Works. 1.3 This section covers the accounting procedures in respect of purchase/ acquisition and disposal of Fixed Assets, including acquisition by way of gift or donation. This chapter also covers the accounting for Depreciation on all the Fixed Assets of the ULBs including those covered by section on "Public Works". 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Purchase of Fixed Assets Debit 410-40-01 Fixed assets- Water treatment plant Credit 350-10-01 To Suppliers Explanation: Entry will be recorded on purchase of fixed assets. In this example, it is assumed that the purchase of water treatment plant is on credit basis. The cost of an item of FA will comprise its purchase price, duties, other levies, carriage inwards, installation charges incurred, any other Expenses specifically attributable to acquisition of FA. Trade discounts and rebates are to be deducted in arriving at the purchase price. In case the Asset is purchased out of specific Grant, the same Grant Creditor's Account shall be used. Details regarding FA purchased will be recorded in the FA Register include supplier's name, invoice reference number, break-up of cost, date of purchase, date of installation, location of Asset, basis and rate of Depreciation. Trigger Point: Receipt of the Asset and acknowledgement of the same Source Document: Invoice with relevant supporting documents Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, N 119 Register of Movable Propertyand the relevant registers. 2 Payment for Fixed Asset Purchased Debit 350-10-01 Suppliers Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The creditors of the respective FA are paid through Bank. Above entry will be passed when such payment is made. In case the Asset is purchased out of specific Grant, the same Bank Account shall be used. Trigger Point: Issue of Cheque Source Document: Invoice with relevant support documents, Register of bills for payment (N 79) Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 3 Charging of Depreciation Debit 272-40-01 Depreciation- Water treatment plant Credit 411-40-01 To Accumulated Depreciation - Water treatment plant Explanation: The Depreciation will be charged on all assets e.g. water treatment plant, at the rates prescribed. The amount of Depreciation determined will be charged to the Accumulated Depreciation A/c. Depreciation will be charged for either six months or twelve months depending on the date of purchase/ sale. In case assets are purchased out of Grants, a proportionate amount of grant shall also be credited to the Income & Expenditure Account from Grant against Asset A/c. Entries for the same are shown in Section 5.14 Grants. Trigger Point: End of the financial year/ sale of assets Source Document: Calculation Sheet and Fixed Asset Register Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, N 119 Register of Movable Property, and the relevant registers. 4 Creation of Sinking Fund Debit 450-41-01 Bank Account- Special Fund Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: A Sinking Fund may have to be created for the purposes of asset replacement. The following entry will be used to transfer funds from the Main Bank Account to a designated Bank Account. Investments or otherwise will be done from the designated Bank Account. The amount to be transferred may be linked to the amount of Depreciation charged. Entries related to investments out of sinking fund have been shown in Chapter 5.11- Investments. Trigger Point: End of the financial year/ time of transfer of funds Source Document: Calculation Sheet , N 119 Register of Movable Property , N 118 Register of Immovable Property Records updated: Journal book, Ledger 5 Assets held for disposal Debit 410-90-08 Assets under disposal Debit 411-40-01 Accumulated Depreciation Credit 410-40-01 To Fixed assets- Water treatment plant Explanation: In case of Fixed Asset to be sold, the Accounts Department shall compute the Written Down Value for that Fixed Asset. Written Down Value of an Asset is calculated as Cost of the Fixed Asset less Accumulated Depreciation provided till the previous year less Provision for Depreciation made during the year till the month of disposal. This will be transferred to an Account called Assets held under disposal. The relevant amount of accumulated depreciation shall also be debited. Illustration - water treatment plant. Trigger Point: At the time of identification of an Asset to be sold or held for disposal Source Document: Approval letter from Mayor/ Chairman, N 119 Register of Movable Property , N 118 Register of Immovable Property Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, 6 When the Assets are sold Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 350-90-01 To Sale proceeds from assets Explanation: At the time of sale of a FA, the amount received from sale will be credited to the Account sale proceeds from assets Trigger Point: Sale of FA Source Document: N 118 Register of Immovable Property, N 119 Register of Movable Property Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 7 Adjustment entry for assets sold Debit 350-90-01 Sale proceeds from assets Debit 271-10-07 Loss on disposal of Assets-Plant and Machinery Credit 410-90-08 To Assets under disposal Credit 180-30-07 To Profit on disposal of Fixed Asset-Plant and Machinery Explanation: Once all the formalities of sale are completed, the balances under the accounts will be adjusted and difference treated as profit or loss on sale. Example of Water treatment plant has been taken Trigger Point: When the profit or loss on the sale proceeds of assets is ascertained. Source Document: N 118 Register of Immovable Property, N 119 Register of Movable Property, Sale agreement Records updated: Journal book, Ledger,

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.13 Special Transactions
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 Special transactions in case of an ULB could include a. Fixed assets created on behalf of ULBs by an executing agency, from the Funds directly disbursed to them by the Government b. Grants given by ULBs to the School Boards or other undertakings established under an ULB c. Contributions made by ULBs in creation of assets not owned by it. 2. ACCOUNTING ENTRIES CREATION OF FIXED ASSETS BY AN EXECUTING AGENCY 1 Payment by ULB/ Receipt of loan/ Grant/ to the Executing Agency Debit 460-50-02 Advance against Projects Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Credit 331-20-01 To Loans- State Government Credit 320-10-01 To Grants, contributions for specific purposes-11th Finance Commission Explanation: Sometimes fixed assets are created by an executing agency on behalf of a ULB. Funds may flow through the ULB or may be given directly to the executing agency. This can be in the form of loans (e.g. from State Government), Grants (e.g. from 11th Finance Commission) or direct contribution by the ULB. In any case, the Liability to repay loan or recipient of the Grant is the ULB and assets created also belong to the ULB. The Bank should be credited when the payment is made by ULBs. The relevant account shall be credited. Trigger Point: Receipt of intimation from the disbursing agency of disbursement made to the executing agency or payment by the ULB Source Document: Letter of intimation/ Approval letter Recrds updated: Cash Book/ Journal Book, Ledger 2 Capitalisation of Fixed Asset Debit 410-20-01 Fixed assets - Office Building Credit 460-50-02 To Advance against Projects Explanation: Once the Asset has been created e.g. office building and handed over to the ULB, the ULB shall record the Asset as its own and capitalise the amount. Trigger Point: Transfer of asset to the ULB, asset is ready for its intended use Source Document: Letter of intimation, completion certificate Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger, N 118 Register of Immovable Property 3 On transfer of Grant Debit 320-10-01 Grants, contributions for specific purposes-11th Finance Commission Credit 312-12-01 To Grant against Asset Explanation: Once the Asset has been created and handed over to the ULB, the ULB shall record the Asset as its own and capitalise the amount. Along with the capitalisation, if the Asset has been constructed out of Grant, e.g. 11th Finance Commission, the Grant against specific purpose will be credited to Grant against Asset Account. Trigger Point: Transfer of Asset to the ULB, Asset is ready for its intended use Source Document: Letter of intimation, completion certificate Rcords updated: Journal Book, Ledger GRANTS GIVEN BY ULBS TO THE SCHOOL BOARDS OR OTHER UNDERTAKINGS ESTABLISHED UNDER AN ULB 4 Recording of Revenue Grants either as Reimbursement of the Monthly Expenses or towards any other Revenue Expenses Debit 260-10-02 Grants- State Government Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: ULBs may provide Grants to the Municipal School Board or an other undertaking. ULB may provide the following recurring and non-recurring Grants e.g. received from State Government a. Revenue Grant in the form of reimbursement of monthly Expenses of the School Board or other undertakings; b. Revenue Grant for any other revenue expenditure of the Board or other undertakings, etc; c. Capital Grant for purchase/construction/modifications of any assets of the schools or undertakings. The above entry should be passed when the ULB is used just as a chanelising agent for disbursement of grant by the Government and all responsibilities including providing Utilisation Certificate rests with the School. This entry should be distinguished with providing advance out of Grants. In the latter case, Advance A/c should be debited instead of Grants A/c. In case the disbursement is in the nature of loans, the relevant entries are listed in Section 5.17 Loans and Advances. Trigger Point: Transfer of Grant to the undertaking e.g. school, health unit, hospital Source Document: Documents related to payment Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, N 158 Register of Grants to Schools & Other Undertakings CONTRIBUTIONS BY ULBS IN CREATION OF ASSETS NOT OWNED BY IT Contribution by ULB for assets not owned by it Debit 260-20-03 Contributions-Other Government Agencies Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: ULBs may contribute as its share in some projects, which are created by external agencies e.g. to some other Government Agencies e.g. contribution in any infrastructure projects like multi-modal transport. In such cases ULB does not have any ownership rights on the assets. Trigger Point: Transfer of contribution to the undertaking creating the Asset Source Document: Approval letter , Register of bills for payment Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.14 Grants
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 Grants received by the ULB may be Capital Grants or Revenue Grants. These could be either for general purposes or for carrying out specific projects/ schemes. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES REVENUE GRANTS FOR NON-SPECIFIC PURPOSE Receipt of Revenue Grants for a non-specific purpose Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 160-10-06 To Revenue Grant-Revenue Deficit Grant Explanation: When Revenue Grants e.g. Revenue Deficit Grant are received basically for a non-specific purpose, they are recognised as income on their receipt. Trigger Point: Receipt of Grant Source Document: Deposit receipt of Bank Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Form N 75 Grant Register REVENUE GRANTS FOR SPECIFIC PURPOSE 2 Receipt of Revenue Grants for a specific purpose Debit 450-61-01 Bank A/c- Specific Grant Credit 320-20-01 To Grants, contributions for specific purpose- Salary Grant Explanation: When Revenue Grants e.g. Salary Grant are received basically for a specific purpose, they are recognised as Liability on their receipt. Trigger Point: Receipt of Grant Source Document: Deposit receipt of Bank Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Form 75 Grant Register 3 Payment of expenditure from Revenue Grant received for specific purpose Debit 210-10-01 Salaries, wages and bonus-Basic salary Credit 450-61-01 To Bank A/c Specific Grant Explanation: Payment of expenditure from specific Grant Account Trigger Point: Payment of expenditure Source Document: Government order of Grant , Establishment records Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Form N 75 Grant Register 4 Recognising income in respect of revenue expenditure incurred from Specific Grant Debit 320-20-01 Grants, contributions for specific purpose-Salary Grant Credit 160-10-01 To Revenue Grant-Salary Explanation: The specific Revenue Grant e.g. Salary Grant which was treated as a Liability at the time of receipt, has to be now recognised as an income, to the extent revenue expenditure has been booked in the Income & Expenditure Account. Trigger Point: At the end of a certain period, when all specific Grants are recognised as income to the extent of expenditure incurred. Source Document: Calculation sheets and Grant Register Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, Form 75 Grant Register RECEIPT OF CAPITAL GRANT 5 Receipt of Capital Grant Debit 450-61-01 Bank A/c-Grant Funds Credit 320-10-01 To Grants and contributions for specific purposes- 11th Finance Commission Explanation: When Capital Grants are received, they are to be utilised only for that specific purpose. In case it is received in Advance or has not been utilised, it shall remain as a Liability. Illustration - Received from 11th Finance Commission Trigger Point: Receipt of money Source Document: Deposit receipt of Bank Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Form N 75 Grant Register 6 Payment of Advance to contractors/ suppliers Payment of advance to contractors/ suppliers out of Capital Grants will be as per the entries under "Advances to Contractors" in the Section 5.9 on Public Works. The Grant Bank Account shall be used for making payments. 7 Recording bills from contractors Recording of bills as Liability for payment for assets created out of Capital Grants will be as per the entries under "Contractor's payment" in the Section 5.9 on Public Works 8 Payment of bills of contractors Payment of bills booked as Liability for assets created out of Capital Grants will be as per the entries under "Contractor's payment" in the Section 5.9 on Public Works. Entries for receipt of Security Deposit, Earnest Money Deposit, capitalisation of fixed assets shall be same as mentioned in the Section 5.9 on Public Works. 9 Transfer of Capital Grant to Grant against Asset A/c Debit 320-10-01 Grants and contributions for specific purposes-11th Finance Commission Credit 312-12-01 To Grant against fixed assets Explanation: The specific Capital Grant which was treated as a Liability at the time of receipt e.g. 11th Finance Commission, has to be now recognised as a Grant against that specific Asset, to the extent capitalisation has been booked for the asset. Trigger Point: At the end of a certain period or when the Asset is ready/ put to use. Source Document: Calculation sheets , N 142 Work Sheet Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger, Form N 75 Grant Register 10 Transfer of Grant against Asset as Income to the extent of Depreciation Debit 312-12-01 Grant against fixed assets Credit 160-40-01 To Contribution against fixed assets Explanation: The Grant received towards capital assets, has to be transferred as income in proportion to the Depreciation charged on the Asset. Trigger Point: At the year end, the relevant Grant Account shall stand reduced Source Document: Calculation sheets , N 118 Register of Immovable property, N 119 Register of Movable property Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger, RECEIPT OF GRANT AS NODAL AGENCY 11 Receipt of Grant as nodal agency Receipt of Grant as a nodal agency shall be booked as a separate Grant and credited to a separate Bank Account 12 Payment of Advance for projects from Grants received as nodal agency Debit 460-50-02 Advances against projects Credit 450-61-01 To Bank A/c-Grant Funds Explanation: When Advance is paid out of specific Grants, the concerned head of Account shall be debited Trigger Point: Payment from Bank Source Document: Payment records , Approval letter Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Form N 75 Grant Register 13 Expenditure on projects for which the ULB is an implementing/ nodal agency Debit 460-80-04 Scheme Expenses Credit 460-50-02 To Advances against projects Explanation: ULBs may receive the bills for payment either directly from the contractor's or from the implementing agency. The Expenses incurred against Scheme to which the Grant is received by the ULB shall be taken to a separate Control A/c to accumulate the expenditure incurred. Trigger Point: Receipt of bills from the contractor's and thus booking of Expenses Source Document: Bills of contractor's/ MB records, Work Sheet (N 142) Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 14 Transfer of expenditure incurred for which the ULB is an implementing/ nodal agency Debit 320-30-01 Grants and contributions for specific purposes Credit 460-80-04 To Scheme Expenses Explanation: On completion of the scheme or at the period ends, the total amount of Grant received for specific purpose, as a nodal agency shall be adjusted to the extent of amounts utilised under the project. Trigger Point: End of the project/ accounting period Source Document: Calculation sheets Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger, Form 75 Grant Register 15 Repayment of unutilised specific Grant Debit 320-30-01 Grants and contributions for specific purposes Credit 350-40-07 To Refund payable for specific Grants Explanation: Specific Grants are normally to be used within a period specified in the Government sanction order. Generally the Specific Grants shall be refunded in the following circumstances: (cid:183) On expiry of the period for utilisation of the Grant as per sanction order (cid:183) The total amount utilised out of the Grant is lesser than the amount of the Grant received. Trigger Point: End of the project/ accounting period Source Document: Calculation sheets, Form N 75Grant Register Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger, 16 On payment of unutilised specific Grant Debit 350-40-07 Refund payable for specific Grants Credit 450-61-01 To Bank A/c-Grant Funds Explanation: On payment of the Liability created above, the designated Bank Account shall be credited Trigger Point: On payment Source Document: Payment records, Form N 75 Grant Register Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.15 Borrowings
text: I INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section deals with representative entries related to borrowings by the ULB. On sanction by the State Government, the ULB may borrow Funds, either by issuing debentures, or other wise, for the purposes specified in the approval. These borrowings are generally secured by movable and immovable properties vested in the ULB. Unless specifically approved by the State Government, the loans cannot be used for any purpose other than that specified in the approval. 1.2 Where the terms of sanction provide for the establishment of a Sinking Fund for the repayment of loan, the ULB shall establish it and credit to it, funds from the Municipal Fund. Similarly, if the terms provide for a creation of an Escrow Account for repayment of loans, the ULB shall create it and credit to it, the income earmarked for this. The purpose of establishment of a Sinking Fund or an Escrow Account is to accumulate money, including interest, adequate to repay the loan as per the repayment schedule. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 When Loans Are Received/ Debentures/ Bonds are raised Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c-Loan Credit 330-20-01 To Secured loans-State Government Credit 331-20-01 To Unsecured loans-State Government Explanation: When loans are received by Municipality e.g. from State Government, others, the above entry is passed. Details of loans such as rate of interest, tenure, repayment schedule, interest payment dates etc. need to be maintained in a Loan Register. On receipt of loans in the form of debentures or bonds, the respective Account will be debited. When the loans are being re-paid, a reverse entry shall be passed. Trigger Point: Receipt of Funds Source Document: Loan application/ Sanction letter Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, N 72 Loan Register 2 When Interest on Loan becomes due Debit 240-20-01 Interest and Finance Charges-State Government Credit 350-12-02 To Interest accrued and due Credit 360-20-01 To Provision for interest Explanation: Above entry is passed to account for interest Expenses as and when they fall due, as per the loan agreement e.g. with State Government. The amount due is recognised and transferred to the interest payable Account. Amount for Interest accrued and not due shall be credited to a Provision for Interest Account Trigger Point: Due date/ financial year end Source Document: Loan application/ Sanction letter, N 72 Loan Register Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 3 When Interest is paid Debit 350-12-02 Interest accrued and due Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: This entry will be passed at time of actual payment of the interest to the lender. Any interest amount, which has accrued but has not been paid to the lender, will appear as part of interest payable at the year end. Trigger Point: Payment of interest Source Document: N 72 Loan Register Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 4 Creation of Sinking Fund for repayment of loan Debit 290-10-01 Transfer to Reserve Funds Credit 311-50-01 To Sinking Fund Explanation: Where the terms of sanction for raising loan or issuing debenture provides for establishment of a Sinking Fund, an amount stipulated in the terms of sanction shall be transferred to the Sinking Fund from the Municipal Fund at the end of the year. In case the fund is allocated from any other Fund, the same shall be debited. Trigger Point: End of period Source Document: Loan Register, Calculation Sheet Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 5 Transfer of Funds from Main Bank Account to Sinking Fund Account Debit 450-41-01 Bank A/c-Sinking Fund Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c-Main Account Explanation: The amount so transferred to the Sinking Fund from the Municipal Fund shall be deposited in a separate Bank Account. This Bank Account can be kept with the ULB or maintained by the Collector or DLB on behalf of the ULB. Trigger Point: Transfer of amount Source Document: Transfer documents Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 6 Entries related to Sinking Fund Investments Entries related to Sinking Fund Investments, accrual of interest, sale if investments, etc. are covered in Section 5.11-Investments.

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.16 Lease and Hire Purchase
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section contains the illustrative transaction entries for transactions relating to fixed assets purchased or sold under hire purchase and leases by ULBs. 1.2 Accounting of leases and hire purchase transactions is divided into the following situations: (cid:110) Finance lease when ULB is lessee. (cid:110) Operating lease when ULB is lessor. (cid:110) Hire Purchase when ULB is buyer. (cid:110) Hire Purchase when ULB is seller. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES FINANCE LEASE WHEN ULB IS LESSEE 1 Fixed assets under finance lease Debit 410-40-01 Fixed assets-Water treatment plant Debit 470-30-01 Interest control payable-Leases Credit 350-80-04 To Lease charges payable Explanation: The Asset shall be capitalised as and when the Asset is ready/ put to use or as per the agreement the Asset has been delivered. 'Interest control payable Leases Account' is in the nature of a control Account, which represents an equivalent amount of unpaid interest portion included in the 'Lease Charges Payable'. The balance in this Account gets reduced at the period ends by accounting for the finance charges portion of the lease payment dues for the period. Trigger Point: Purchase of Asset/Asset is ready/Put to use Source Document: Lease agreement Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, N119 Register of Movable Property 2 Payment of periodic lease charges Debit 350-80-04 Lease charges payable Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: Payment of periodic lease charges to the lessor shall be carried out as per lease agreement Trigger Point: Payment of lease charges on the due date Source Document: Payment documents, Lease agreement, N 119 Register of Movable properties Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 3 Adjustment of Finance charges Debit 240-60-01 Interest on leases Credit 470-30-01 To Interest control payable-Leases Explanation: The portion of finance charges out of the lease payments due for the current period shall be identified at the period end and accounted as finance charges for the period. Trigger Point: End of period/ lease period Source Document: Calculation sheets/ Statement of lessor, Lease agreement N 119 Register of Movable properties Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 4 Accrual of lease rentals Debit 431-40-06 Receivables for lease rentals Credit 130-80-03 To Other rents-lease rentals Explanation: The lease rentals due for the period shall be accrued in accordance with the lease agreement. Trigger Point: End of period/ lease period/ As per lease agreement Source Document: Calculation sheets, Lease agreement Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 5 Receipt of lease rentals Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 431-40-06 To Receivables for lease rentals Explanation: The lease rentals shall be received in accordance with the lease agreement. The above entry shall be passed at the time of receipt of the lease rentals. Trigger Point: Receipt of lease rentals Source Document: Receipt documents Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger HIRE PURCHASE WHEN ULB IS BUYER 6 Down payment against purchase of assets Debit 460-40-04 Advance for HP assets Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: The HP agreement may have clauses for down payments either before or after delivery or receipt of the Asset. Trigger Point: Payment of Advance Source Document: Payment records/ Hire purchase agreement Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 7 Purchase of fixed assets through hire purchase Debit 410-40-01 Fixed Asset-Water treatment plant Debit 470-30-02 Interest control payable Hire purchase Credit 330-60-01 To Hire purchase loans Credit 460-40-04 To Advance for HP assets Explanation: Purchase of fixed assets e.g. water treatment plant on hire purchase and also adjustment of down payment Trigger Point: Purchase of Asset Source Document: HP agreement Records updated: Journal book, Ledger, Form N 119 Register of Movable Property 8 Payment of periodic hire purchase instalments Debit 330-60-01 Hire purchase loans Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: Payment of periodic HP instalments Trigger Point: Payment of instalment Source Document: Payment records/ Hire purchase agreement , Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger, Form N 119 Register of Movable Property 9 Adjustment of finance charges Debit 240-60-02 Interest on Hire purchase Credit 470-30-02 To Interest control payable-Hire purchase Explanation: The portion of interest out of the 'HP payable' due for the period under reporting shall be identified at the period ends and accounted as 'finance charges for that period. Trigger Point: Period end/ end of HP period Source Document: Calculation sheets/ HP agreement Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger HIRE PURCHASE WHEN ULB IS SELLER 10 Down payment received against sale of assets Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 350-80-06 To HP Advance received Explanation: The HP agreement may have clauses for down payments either before or after delivery or receipt of the Asset. Trigger Point: Receipt of Advance Source Document: Receipt records/ HP Agreement Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 11 Sale of fixed assets through hire purchase Debit 350-80-06 HP Advance received Debit 460-80-01 HP instalments receivable Credit 410-40-01 To Fixed Asset-Water treatment plant Credit 350-80-05 To Hire Purchase Interest Receivable Control Account Explanation: Sale of fixed assets e.g. water treatment plant on hire purchase and also adjustment of down payment Trigger Point: Sale of Asset Source Document: HP agreement, Form N 119 Register of Movable Property Records updated: Journal book, Ledger 12 Receipt of periodic hire purchase instalments Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 460-80-01 To HP instalments receivable Explanation: Receipt of periodic HP instalments as per HP agreement Trigger Point: Receipt of instalment Source Document: Receipt records/ HP Agreement Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 13 Adjustment of finance charges Debit 350-80-05 Hire Purchase Interest Receivable Control Account Credit 171-80-01 To Interest on Hire Purchase Explanation: The portion of interest out of the HP interest control A/c due for the period under reporting shall be identified at the period ends and accounted as interest for that period. Trigger Point: Period end/ end of HP period Source Document: Calculation sheets/ HP agreement Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.17 Loans and Advances
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 This section deals with the illustrative transaction entries relating to loans given to others including any boards/ undertakings set up by the ULB. ULBs may give loans out of General Funds or out of Grants received for long-term projects if the terms and conditions of the Grant provide for the same. In such cases, interests earned on the loans and advances lent out of the specific Project Grant shall be credited to these Grants Account. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Disbursement of loan Debit 460-30-01 Loan to others Credit 450-21-01 To Bank A/c Explanation: On disbursement of loan to the entity/ undertaking. In case of recovery of loan or if it is through instalments, a reverse entry shall be passed Trigger Point: At the time of payment Source Document: Payment records, Form N 72 Register of Loans to other Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 2 Interest due on loans Debit 431-40-02 Interest accrued Credit 171-30-01 To Interest on Loans and advances to Others Explanation: On Accrual of interest on loan and advances Trigger Point: As per loan agreement, when the interest becomes due Source Document: Loan agreement, Form N 72 Register of Loans to other Records updated: Journal Book, Ledger 3 Receipt of interest due Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 431-40-02 To Interest accrued Explanation: On receipt of interest accrued on loan and advances Trigger Point: Receipt of interest Source Document: Receipt records, Form N 72 Register of Loans to other Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger

title: Transaction Entries
heading: 5.18 Municipal School
text: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 Education is one of the services of the ULB and is carried out by ULBs directly or through separate boards set up specifically for the purpose. This section contains the recommended accounting system for transactions relating to Municipal School Board. School Boards shall maintain independent books of accounts for recording transactions relating to the board. 2 ACCOUNTING ENTRIES 1 Receipt of fees and fines Debit 450-21-01 Bank A/c Credit 140-40-02 To Tuition fees Explanation: On receipt of fees and fines and depositing them in the Bank Account Trigger Point: Receipt of fees Source Document: Receipt records, Form N 105 Summary of daily collection Records updated: Cash Book, Ledger 2 Other transaction related to School Boards The other transaction relating to a school shall be same as illustrated in the other sections.
